definition: The mismatch between declared and actual FX regimes affects approximately 40-50% of countries (Reinhart-Rogoff 2004), likely worsening to 50-55% post-COVID. Managed regimes that claim to float produce 30-60% discrete devaluations versus 15-25% continuous adjustment in credible floaters. India (RBI suppressing USD/INR vol to 3-4% vs. peer-implied 7-9%) is the clearest current case.
evidence: Post-COVID increase to 50-55% is directional judgment, not formal reclassification
India: RBI suppresses USD/INR vol to 3-4% vs. peer-implied 7-9%
Managed regimes produce 30-60% discrete devaluations vs. 15-25% continuous adjustment in credible floaters
open_questions: Which additional countries have widened de jure/de facto gaps post-COVID?
Does the mismatch rate predict crisis severity or just crisis occurrence?
What triggers the shift from managed to disorderly adjustment?
